text.NRSV: Then Judith came in and reclined; and Holofernes’ heart was ravished with her and his passion was aroused, for he had been waiting for an opportunity to seduce her from the day he first saw her.

text.KJV: 12:20 And Olofernes tooke great delight in her, & dranke much more wine, then he had drunke at any time in one day, since he was borne.
text.DRV: 12:20 And Holofernes was made pleasant toward her, & drank wine exceeding much, so much as he had never drunk in his life?
text.NABRE: 12:20 Holofernes, charmed by her, drank a great quantity of wine, more than he had ever drunk on any day since he was born.
text.NRSV: Holofernes was greatly pleased with her, and drank a great quantity of wine, much more than he had ever drunk in any one day since he was born.

text.KJV: 13:1 Now when the euening was come, his seruants made haste to depart, and Bagoas shut his tent without, and dismissed the waiters from the presence of his lord, and they went to their beds: for they were all weary, because the feast had bene long.
text.DRV: 13:1 And when it was waxen late, his servants made haste to their lodgings, and Vagao shut the chamber doors, and went his way.
text.NABRE: 13:1 Chapter 13 - Judith Beheads Holofernes. When it grew late, his servants quickly withdrew. Bagoas closed the tent from the outside and dismissed the attendants from their master’s presence. They went off to their beds, for they were all tired because the banquet had lasted so long.
text.NRSV: When evening came, his slaves quickly withdrew. Bagoas closed the tent from outside and shut out the attendants from his master’s presence; and they went to bed, for they all were weary because the banquet had lasted so long.

text.KJV: 13:2 And Iudeth was left alone in the tent, and Olofernes lying along vpon his bed, for hee was filled with wine.
text.DRV: 13:2 And they were all overladen with wine.
text.NABRE: 13:2 Judith was left alone in the tent with Holofernes, who lay sprawled on his bed, for he was drunk with wine.
text.NRSV: So Judith was left alone in the tent, with Holofernes stretched out on his bed, for he was dead drunk.

text.KJV: 13:3 Now Iudeth had commanded her maide to stand without her bedchamber, and to waite for her comming forth as she did daily: for she said, she would goe forth to her prayers, and she spake to Bagoas, according to the same purpose.
text.DRV: 13:3 And Judith was alone in the chamber.
text.NABRE: 13:3 Judith had ordered her maidservant to stand outside the bedchamber and to wait, as on the other days, for her to come out; she had said she would be going out for her prayer. She had also said this same thing to Bagoas.
text.NRSV: Now Judith had told her maid to stand outside the bedchamber and to wait for her to come out, as she did on the other days; for she said she would be going out for her prayers.

text.KJV: 13:4 So all went forth, and none was left in the bedchamber, neither little, nor great. Then Iudeth standing by his bed, said in her heart: O Lord God of all power, looke at this present vpon the workes of mine hands for the exaltation of Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 13:4 Moreover Holofernes lay in his bed, fast asleep with very much drunkenness.
text.NABRE: 13:4 When all had departed, and no one, small or great, was left in the bedchamber, Judith stood by Holofernes’ bed and prayed silently, “O Lord, God of all might, in this hour look graciously on the work of my hands for the exaltation of Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: So everyone went out, and no one, either small or great, was left in the bedchamber. Then Judith, standing beside his bed, said in her heart, “O Lord God of all might, look in this hour upon the work of my hands for the exaltation of Jerusalem.”

text.KJV: 13:6 Then she came to the pillar of the bed, which was at Olofernes head, and tooke downe his fauchin from thence,
text.DRV: 13:6 And Judith stood before the bed, praying with tears, & with moving of her lips in silence,
text.NABRE: 13:6 She went to the bedpost near the head of Holofernes, and taking his sword from it,
text.NRSV: She went up to the bedpost near Holofernes’ head, and took down his sword that hung there.

text.KJV: 13:7 And approched to his bed, and tooke hold of the haire of his head, and said, Strengthen mee, O Lord God of Israel, this day.
text.DRV: 13:7 saying: Confirm me o Lord God of Israel and in this hour have respect to the works of my hands, that as thou hast promised, thou mayst advance Jerusalem thy city: and I may bring to pass that which I believing that it may be done by thee, have purposed.
text.NABRE: 13:7 she drew close to the bed, grasped the hair of his head, and said, “Strengthen me this day, Lord, God of Israel!”
text.NRSV: She came close to his bed and took hold of the hair of his head, and said, “Give me strength today, O Lord God of Israel!”

text.KJV: 13:8 And she smote twise vpon his necke with all her might, and she tooke away his head from him,
text.DRV: 13:8 And when she had said these things, she went to the pillar, that was at his bed's head, and his sword that hung tied on it, she loosed.
text.NABRE: 13:8 Then with all her might she struck his neck twice and cut off his head.
text.NRSV: Then she struck his neck twice with all her might, and cut off his head.

text.KJV: 13:9 And tumbled his body downe from the bed, and pulled downe the canopy from the pillars, and anon after she went forth, and gaue Olofernes his head to her maide.
text.DRV: 13:9 And when she had drawn it out, she took him by the hair of his head, and said: Confirm me, o Lord God, in this hour,
text.NABRE: 13:9 She rolled his body off the bed and took the canopy from its posts. Soon afterward, she came out and handed over the head of Holofernes to her maid,
text.NRSV: Next she rolled his body off the bed and pulled down the canopy from the posts; soon afterward she went out and gave Holofernes’ head to her maid,

text.KJV: 13:10 And she put it in her bag of meate, so they twaine went together according to their custome vnto prayer, and when they passed the campe, they compassed the valley, and went vp the mountaine of Bethulia, and came to the gates thereof.
text.DRV: 13:10 and she struck twice upon his neck, and cut off his head, and took his canopy from the pillars, and rolled aside his body a trunk.
text.NABRE: 13:10 who put it into her food bag. Then the two went out together for prayer as they were accustomed to do. Judith and Her Maid Return to Bethulia. They passed through the camp, and skirting that valley, went up the mountain to Bethulia, and approached its gates.
text.NRSV: who placed it in her food bag. Then the two of them went out together, as they were accustomed to do for prayer; they passed through the camp, circled around the valley, and went up the mountain to Bethulia, and came to its gates.

text.KJV: 13:15 So she tooke the head out of the bag, and shewed it, and said vnto them, Behold the head of Olofernes the chiefe captaine of the armie of Assur, and behold the canopy wherein he did lie in his drunkennesse, and the Lord hath smitten him by the hand of a woman.
text.DRV: 13:15 And they ran all to meet her, from the least to the greatest: because they hoped not that now she would come.
text.NABRE: 13:15 Then she took the head out of the bag, showed it to them, and said: “Here is the head of Holofernes, the ranking general of the Assyrian forces, and here is the canopy under which he lay in his drunkenness. The Lord struck him down by the hand of a female!
text.NRSV: She took the head out of the bag and showed it to them, and said, “See! Here is the head of Holofernes, the general of the Assyrian army, and here is the canopy under which he lay in his drunkenness. The Lord has struck him down by the hand of a woman.”

text.KJV: 13:18 Then said Ozias vnto her, O daughter, blessed art thou of the most high God, aboue all the women vpon the earth, and blessed be the Lord God, which hath created the heauens, and the earth, which hath directed thee to the cutting off of the head of the chiefe of our enemies.
text.DRV: 13:18 and in me his handmaid he hath fulfilled his mercy, which he promised to the house of Israel: and he hath killed by my hand the enemy of his people this night.
text.NABRE: 13:18 Then Uzziah said to her, “Blessed are you, daughter, by the Most High God, above all the women on earth; and blessed be the Lord God, the creator of heaven and earth, who guided your blow at the head of the leader of our enemies.
text.NRSV: Then Uzziah said to her, “O daughter, you are blessed by the Most High God above all other women on earth; and blessed be the Lord God, who created the heavens and the earth, who has guided you to cut off the head of the leader of our enemies.”

text.KJV: 14:1 Then saide Iudeth vnto them, Heare me now, my brethren, & take this head, and hang it vpon the highest place of your walles.
text.DRV: 14:1 And Judith said to all the people: Hear me brethren, hang ye this head upon our walls:
text.NABRE: 14:1 V. Victory and Thanksgiving Chapter 14 - Judith’s Plan of Attack. Then Judith said to them: “Listen to me, my brothers and sisters. Take this head and hang it on the parapet of your wall.
text.NRSV: Then Judith said to them, “Listen to me, my brothers. Take this head and hang it upon the parapet of your wall.”

text.KJV: 14:2 And so soone as the morning shall appeare and the Sunne shal come forth vpon the earth, take you euery one his weapons, and goe forth euery valiant man out of the city, & set you a captaine ouer them, as though you would goe downe into the field toward the watch of the Assyrians, but goe not downe.
text.DRV: 14:2 and it shall be, when the sun shall rise, let every man take his armour, & issue ye forth with violence, not that you go down beneath, but as it were invading violently.
text.NABRE: 14:2 At daybreak, when the sun rises on the earth, each of you seize your weapons, and let all the able-bodied men rush out of the city under command of a captain, as if about to go down into the valley against the Assyrian patrol, but without going down.
text.NRSV: “As soon as day breaks and the sun rises on the earth, each of you take his weapons, and let every able-bodied man go out of the city; and appoint a captain over them, as if you were going down into the plain against the Assyrian outpost; only do not go down.”

text.KJV: 14:3 Then they shal take their armour, and shal goe into their campe, and raise vp the captaines of the armie of Assur, and they shall runne to the tent of Olofernes, but shall not finde him, then feare shall fall vpon them, and they shall flee before your face.
text.DRV: 14:3 Then the watchmen must of necessity run to raise up their Prince to battle.
text.NABRE: 14:3 The Assyrians will seize their weapons and hurry to their camp to awaken the generals of the army. When they run to the tent of Holofernes and do not find him, panic will seize them, and they will flee before you.
text.NRSV: “Then they will seize their weapons and go into their camp and rouse the officers of the Assyrian army. They will rush into the tent of Holofernes and will not find him. Then panic will come over them, and they will flee before you.”

text.KJV: 14:6 Then they called Achior out of the house of Ozias, and when hee was come, and saw the head of Olofernes in a mans hand, in the assembly of the people, he fell downe on his face, and his spirit failed.
text.DRV: 14:6 Then Achior seeing the power, that God of Israel wrought, forsaking the rite of gentility, believed God, and circumcised the flesh of his prepuce, and was joined to the people of Israel, and all the succession of his kindred until this present day.
text.NABRE: 14:6 So they called Achior from the house of Uzziah. When he came and saw the head of Holofernes in the hand of one of the men in the assembly of the people, he collapsed in a faint.
text.NRSV: When they had summoned Achior from the house of Uzziah, he came and saw the head of Holofernes in the hand of one of the men in the assembly of the people; and he fell down on his face and fainted.

text.KJV: 14:10 And when Achior had seene all that the God of Israel had done, hee beleeued in God greatly, and circumcised the foreskinne of his flesh, and was ioyned vnto the house of Israel vnto this day.
text.DRV: 14:10 For no man durst by knocking, or entering, to open the chamber of the chief of the Assyrians.
text.NABRE: 14:10 Now Achior, seeing all that the God of Israel had done, believed firmly in God. He circumcised the flesh of his foreskin and he has been united with the house of Israel to the present day.
text.NRSV: When Achior saw all that the God of Israel had done, he believed firmly in God. So he was circumcised, and joined the house of Israel, as he is to this day.

text.KJV: 14:11 And assoone as the morning arose, they hanged the head of Olofernes vpon the wall, and euery man took his weapons, and they went foorth by bandes vnto the straits of the mountaine.
text.DRV: 14:11 But when his Dukes and tribunes were come, and all the chief of the army of the king of the Assyrians, they said to the chamberlains:
text.NABRE: 14:11 Panic in the Assyrian Camp. At daybreak they hung the head of Holofernes on the wall. Then all the Israelite men took up their weapons and went out by groups to the mountain passes.
text.NRSV: As soon as it was dawn, they hung the head of Holofernes on the wall. Then all the Israelite men took their weapons, and they went out in companies to the mountain passes.

text.KJV: 14:12 But when the Assyrians sawe them, they sent to their leaders, which came to their Captaines, and tribunes, and to euery one of their rulers.
text.DRV: 14:12 Go in, and awake him, because the mice issuing out of their holes, have presumed to provoke us to battle.
text.NABRE: 14:12 When the Assyrians saw them, they notified their commanders, who, in turn, went to their generals, their division leaders, and all their other leaders.
text.NRSV: When the Assyrians saw them, they sent word to their commanders, who then went to the generals and the captains and all their other officers.

text.KJV: 14:14 Then went in Bagoas, and knocked at the doore of the tent: for he thought that he had slept with Iudeth.
text.DRV: 14:14 But when with the sense of his ears he perceived no motion of person lying, he came near to the curtain, and lifting it up, and seeing the body without the head of Holofernes weltered in his blood lie upon the ground, cried out in a loud voice with weeping, and rent his garments.
text.NABRE: 14:14 So Bagoas went in and knocked at the entry of the tent, presuming that Holofernes was sleeping with Judith.
text.NRSV: So Bagoas went in and knocked at the entry of the tent, for he supposed that Holofernes was sleeping with Judith.

text.KJV: 14:15 But because none answered, he opened it, and went into the bedchamber, and found him cast vpon the floore dead, & his head was taken from him.
text.DRV: 14:15 And going into the tabernacle of Judith, he found her not, and he lept forth to the people,
text.NABRE: 14:15 When no one answered, he parted the curtains, entered the bedchamber, and found him thrown on the floor dead, with his head gone!
text.NRSV: But when no one answered, he opened it and went into the bedchamber and found him thrown down on the platform a dead corpse, with his head cut off and taken away.

text.KJV: 14:18 These slaues haue dealt treacherously, one woman of the Hebrewes hath brought shame vpon the house of king Nabuchodonosor: for behold, Olofernes lieth vpon the ground without a head.
text.DRV: 14:18 And there was made an incomparable cry in the midst of their camp.
text.NABRE: 14:18 “The slaves have duped us! One Hebrew woman has brought shame on the house of King Nebuchadnezzar. Look! Holofernes on the ground—without a head!”
text.NRSV: “The slaves have duped us! One Hebrew woman has brought disgrace on the house of King Nebuchadnezzar. For look, Holofernes is lying on the ground without a head!”

text.KJV: 15:1 And when they that were in the tents heard, they were astonished at the thing that was done.
text.DRV: 15:1 And when all the army heard that Holofernes was beheaded, courage and counsel fell from them, and being shaken with trembling only and fear, they help themselves by flight,
text.NABRE: 15:1 Chapter 15 - On hearing what had happened, those still in their tents were horrified.
text.NRSV: When those in the tents heard it, they were amazed at what had happened.

text.KJV: 15:2 And feare and trembling fell vpon them, so that there was no man that durst abide in the sight of his neighbour, but rushing out altogether, they fled into euery way of the plaine, and of the hill countrey.
text.DRV: 15:2 so that none spake with his neighbour, but hanging the head, all things left behind, they made haste to escape the Hebrews, whom they heard to come armed upon them, flying by the ways of the fields, and the paths of the hills.
text.NABRE: 15:2 Overcome with fear and dread, no one kept ranks any longer. They scattered in all directions, and fled along every path, both through the valley and in the hill country.
text.NRSV: Overcome with fear and trembling, they did not wait for one another; but with one impulse they all rushed out and fled by every path across the plain and through the hill country.

text.KJV: 15:5 Now when the children of Israel heard it, they all fell vpon them with one consent, and slewe them vnto Choba: likewise also they that came from Ierusalem, and from all the hill country, for men had told them what things were done in the campe of their enemies, and they that were in Galaad and in Galile chased them with a great slaughter, vntill they were past Damascus, and the borders thereof.
text.DRV: 15:5 Ozias therefore sent messengers through all the cities and countries of Israel.
text.NABRE: 15:5 On hearing this, all the Israelites, with one accord, attacked them and cut them down as far as Choba. Even those from Jerusalem and the rest of the hill country took part in this, for they too had been notified of the happenings in the camp of their enemy. The Gileadites and the Galileans struck the enemy’s flanks with great slaughter, even beyond Damascus and its borders.
text.NRSV: As soon as the Israelites heard it, with one accord they fell upon the enemy, and cut them down as far as Choba. Those in Jerusalem and all the hill country also came, for they were told what had happened in the camp of the enemy.

text.KJV: 15:8 Then Ioacim the high Priest, and the Ancients of the children of Israel that dwelt in Ierusalem, came to behold the good things that God had shewed to Israel, and to see Iudeth, and to salute her.
text.DRV: 15:8 But they that were returned conquerors to Bethulia, took away with them all things that were theirs, so that there was no number in cattle, and beasts, and all their moveables, that from the least unto the greatest all were made rich of their preys.
text.NABRE: 15:8 Israel Celebrates Judith’s Victory. Then the high priest Joakim and the senate of the Israelites who lived in Jerusalem came to see for themselves the good things that the Lord had done for Israel, and to meet and congratulate Judith.
text.NRSV: Then Joakim the high priest and the council of the Israelites who lived in Jerusalem came to see for themselves the good things that the Lord had done for Israel, and to see Judith and to wish her well.

text.KJV: 15:9 And when they came vnto her, they blessed her with one accord, and said vnto her, Thou art the exaltation of Ierusalem: thou art the great glory of Israel: thou art the great reioycing of our nation.
text.DRV: 15:9 And Ioachim the high Priest came from Jerusalem into Bethulia with all his ancients to see Judith.
text.NABRE: 15:9 When they came to her, all with one accord blessed her, saying: “You are the glory of Jerusalem! You are the great pride of Israel! You are the great boast of our nation!
text.NRSV: When they met her they all blessed her with one accord and said to her, “You are the glory of Jerusalem, you are the great pride of Israel, you are the highest honor of our people.”

text.KJV: 15:11 And the people spoiled the campe, the space of thirty dayes, and they gaue vnto Iudeth Olofernes his tent, and all his plate, and beds, and vessels, and all his stuffe: and she tooke it, and laide it on her mule, and made ready her carts, and laid them thereon.
text.DRV: 15:11 because thou hast done manfully, and thy heart was strengthened, for that thou had loved chastity, and after thy husband not known any other: therefore also the hand of our Lord hath strengthened thee, and therefore shall thou be blessed for ever.
text.NABRE: 15:11 For thirty days all the people plundered the camp, giving Judith the tent of Holofernes, with all his silver, his beds, his dishes, and all his furniture. She took them and loaded her mule, hitched her carts, and loaded these things on them.
text.NRSV: The people plundered the camp for thirty days. They gave Judith the tent of Holofernes and all his silver dinnerware and his beds and his bowls and all his furniture.

text.KJV: 15:12 Then all the women of Israel ran together to see her, and blessed her, and made a dance among them for her: and shee tooke branches in her hand, & gaue also to the women that were with her.
text.DRV: 15:12 And all the people said: So be it, so be it.
text.NABRE: 15:12 All the women of Israel gathered to see her, and they blessed her and performed a dance in her honor. She took branches in her hands and distributed them to the women around her,
text.NRSV: Then all the women of Israel gathered to see her, and blessed her, and some of them performed a dance for her.

text.KJV: 16:1 Then Iudeth began to sing this thankesgiuing in all Israel, and all the people sang after her this song of praise.
text.DRV: 16:1 Then sang Judith this song to our Lord, saying:
text.NABRE: 16:1 Chapter 16 Judith’s Hymn of Deliverance - And Judith sang: “Strike up a song to my God with tambourines, sing to the Lord with cymbals; Improvise for him a new song, exalt and acclaim his name.
text.NRSV: Judith said, Begin a song to my God with tambourines, sing to my Lord with cymbals. Raise to him a new psalm; exalt him, and call upon his name.

text.KJV: 16:2 And Iudeth said, Begin vnto my God with timbrels, sing vnto my Lord with cymbals: tune vnto him a newe Psalme: exalt him, & cal vpon his name.
text.DRV: 16:2 Begin ye to our Lord in timbrels, sing ye to our Lord in cymbals, tune to him a new Psalm, rejoice, and invocate his name.
text.NABRE: 16:2 For the Lord is a God who crushes wars; he sets his encampment among his people; he delivered me from the hands of my pursuers.
text.NRSV: For the Lord is a God who crushes wars; he sets up his camp among his people; he delivered me from the hands of my pursuers.

text.KJV: 16:17 Woe to the nations that rise vp against my kinred: the Lord almighty will take vengeance of them in the day of iudgement in putting fire & wormes in their flesh, and they shall feele them and weepe for euer.
text.DRV: 16:17 Let every creature of thine serve thee: because thou saidst, and they were made: thou didst send thy spirit, and they were created, and there is none that can resist thy voice.
text.NABRE: 16:17 “Woe to the nations that rise against my people! the Lord Almighty will requite them; in the day of judgment he will punish them: He will send fire and worms into their flesh, and they will weep and suffer forever.”
text.NRSV: Woe to the nations that rise up against my people! The Lord Almighty will take vengeance on them in the day of judgment; he will send fire and worms into their flesh; they shall weep in pain forever.

text.KJV: 16:18 Now assoone as they entred into Ierusalem, they worshipped the Lord, and assoone as the people were purified, they offered their burnt offerings, and their free offerings, and their gifts.
text.DRV: 16:18 The mountains with the waters shall be moved with the foundations: the rocks shall melt as wax before thy face.
text.NABRE: 16:18 When they arrived at Jerusalem, they worshiped God. As soon as the people were purified, they offered their burnt offerings, voluntary offerings, and donations.
text.NRSV: When they arrived at Jerusalem, they worshiped God. As soon as the people were purified, they offered their burnt offerings, their freewill offerings, and their gifts.

text.KJV: 16:21 After this time, euery one returned to his owne inheritance, and Iudeth went to Bethulia, and remained in her owne possession, and was in her time honourable in all the countrey.
text.DRV: 16:21 For he will give fire, and worms into their flesh, that they may be burnt, and may feel for ever.
text.NABRE: 16:21 The Renown and Death of Judith. When those days were over, all of them returned to their inheritance. Judith went back to Bethulia and remained on her estate. For the rest of her life she was renowned throughout the land.
text.NRSV: After this they all returned to their own inheritances. Judith went to Bethulia, and remained on her estate.

text.KJV: 16:23 But she encreased more and more in honour, and waxed olde in her husbands house, being an hundred and fiue yeeres olde, and made her maide free, so shee died in Bethulia: and they buried her in the caue of her husband Manasses.
text.DRV: 16:23 Moreover Judith offered for an anathema of oblivion all the instruments of war of Holofernes, which the people gave her, and the canopy that herself had taken away out of his chamber.
text.NABRE: 16:23 Her fame continued to increase, and she lived in the house of her husband, reaching the advanced age of one hundred and five. She set her maid free. And when she died in Bethulia, they buried her in the cave of her husband, Manasseh;
text.NRSV: She became more and more famous, and grew old in her husband’s house, reaching the age of one hundred five years. She set her maid free.

text.KJV: 16:24 And the house of Israel lamented her seauen dayes, and before shee dyed, she did distribute her goods to all them that are neerest of kinred to Manasses her husband: and to them that were the neerest of her kinred.
text.DRV: 16:24 And the people was pleasant according to the face of saints, and for three months the joy of this victory was celebrated with Judith.
text.NABRE: 16:24 and the house of Israel mourned her for seven days. Before she died, she distributed her property to the relatives of her husband, Manasseh, and to her own relatives.
text.NRSV: She died in Bethulia, and they buried her in the cave of her husband Manasseh.

text.KJV: 16:25 And there was none that made the children of Israel any more afraide, in the dayes of Iudeth, nor a long time after her death.
text.DRV: 16:25 And after those days every man returned into his house, and Judith was made great in Bethulia, and she was more glorious to all the land of Israel.
text.NABRE: 16:25 During the lifetime of Judith and for a long time after her death, no one ever again spread terror among the Israelites.
text.NRSV: And no one ever again spread terror among the Israelites during the lifetime of Judith, or for a long time after her death.
